incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols on first attempt

steps.text: Check the table. Chips has 10 votes, and each symbol stands for 2 votes.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The table shows 10 for Chips. Each symbol equals 2 votes. How many 2s are in 10?

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong number of symbols three or more times — system models the answer

params.description: System places 5 symbols in Chips column, one at a time

steps.text: This is tricky, so let me show you. Chips has 10 votes. Each symbol represents 2 votes. 10 divided by 2 equals 5, so we place 5 symbols. See how that works?
